cases.description: Declared point lookup returns the expected single row by primary key.
seed_sql: INSERT INTO public.tenants (tenant_id, tenant_name) VALUES ('tenant-a', 'Tenant A')
INSERT INTO public.tenants (tenant_id, tenant_name) VALUES ('tenant-b', 'Tenant B')
INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-001', 'tenant-a', 'ext-001', 'Alpha', NULL, 100)
INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-002', 'tenant-a', 'ext-002', 'Beta', 'open', 125)
INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-003', 'tenant-b', 'ext-003', 'Gamma', 'closed', 90)
cases.sql: SELECT deal_id, deal_name FROM public.deals WHERE deal_id = 'deal-001'

INSERT INTO public.tenants (tenant_id, tenant_name) VALUES ('tenant-b', 'Tenant B')

INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-002', 'tenant-a', 'ext-002', 'Beta', 'open', 125)
INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-003', 'tenant-b', 'ext-003', 'Gamma', 'closed', 90)
cases.sql: SELECT deal_id FROM public.deals WHERE status IN ('open', NULL)

cases.description: BETWEEN over a declared numeric column refuses nonnumeric scalar bounds instead of coercing silently.

INSERT INTO public.deals (deal_id, tenant_id, external_key, deal_name, status, amount) VALUES ('deal-001', 'tenant-a', 'ext-001', 'Alpha', NULL, 100)
